claim_boundary: TICKET119 is a preregistered finite persistence test. A pass does not prove eventual positivity or Twin Prime; a failure refutes only the frozen 8M-to-16M dyadic-persistence claim.

preregistration.holdout_scope: The 16M canonical adjacent-dyadic-pair endpoint statistic has not been evaluated. TICKET-118 exposed only the corresponding 8M result before this registration.

partition_rule.shells: List every nonempty truncated dyadic outer-divisor shell intersecting [U+1, floor((X+2)/(V+1))] in increasing shell order.
partition_rule.groups: Pair shell indices (0,1), (2,3), and so on; if the shell count is odd, retain the final shell as a singleton.
partition_rule.denominator_policy: Use the same partition for every right Farey denominator q=2,...,32.
partition_rule.optimization_ban: Do not search partitions, signs, subsets, weights, exponents, cutoffs, denominator-specific groups, or alternate primary endpoints after reading the 16M coefficients.

primary_endpoint.failure_condition: less than or equal to zero
primary_endpoint.meaning: A pass is one additional finite closure. A failure refutes DyadicPersistenceFromEightMillion for the frozen rule at 16M. Neither outcome proves or disproves the Twin Prime Conjecture.

registered_reference.use: The 8M row is a disclosed reference only. It does not set a fitted exponent, threshold, partition, or 16M decision rule.

secondary_endpoints: canonical adverse budget divided by known_without_type_ii_minor
finite lower expression divided by known_without_type_ii_minor
16M normalized margin compared descriptively with the disclosed 8M normalized margin
canonical numerator budget divided by the fully signed numerator budget
canonical partition compared with the best width-two contiguous partition, reported descriptively without changing the primary endpoint
time-domain, endpoint-profile, phase, and real-Gram reconstruction errors

execution_policy.reporting: Record the first completed 16M result whether it passes or fails. A resource failure is reported as inconclusive and does not permit a changed mathematical rule under this registration.
execution_policy.next_scale: Do not claim a 32M result under this registration. A later scale requires a new public registration after the 16M outcome is recorded.

preregistration.claim_boundary: No 16M endpoint coefficients or result were used to choose this rule or endpoint. This registration tests finite persistence of one frozen bound, not eventual positivity and not any of the four conjectures.

primary_result.interpretation: This is exactly one post-registration 16M finite test. It is not an all-sufficiently-large-X theorem.

scale_comparison.interpretation: The direction comparison is a registered secondary endpoint. It neither fits nor proves a scaling exponent.

twin_canonical_pair_doubling_holdout.proof_boundary: TICKET119 is a preregistered finite persistence test. A pass does not prove eventual positivity or Twin Prime; a failure refutes only the frozen 8M-to-16M dyadic-persistence claim.

attempts.proof_or_counterexample_mode: preserve the independent target during the Twin 16M persistence holdout
attempts.attempt: Preserve the existing infinite target; no Twin scale-persistence result is transferred.
attempts.obstruction: TICKET119 supplies no new target-specific infinite theorem for this problem.
attempts.next_experiment: Continue NonCircularExplicitFormulaKernelPositivity with its own proof and counterexample oracle.
attempts.claim_boundary: No riemann proof and no certified riemann counterexample.

attempts.attempt: Preserve the existing infinite target; no Twin scale-persistence result is transferred.

attempts.proof_or_counterexample_mode: preregistered 16M falsification attempt of the frozen 8M canonical-pair persistence claim
attempts.attempt: Apply the unchanged canonical adjacent-shell rule at the first unseen doubled scale and report the first result without retuning.
attempts.obstruction: Even two consecutive dyadic closures would not prove an eventual bound; a finite failure would refute only this persistence route, not Twin Prime.
attempts.next_experiment: Use the 16M group profile to state a result-independent denominator-summed bilinear lemma, or preregister the next dyadic falsification scale without changing the rule.
attempts.claim_boundary: No Twin Prime proof and no certified last-twin counterexample.
